Avoidable mortality: exploratory overview

Deaths aged 0–74, preventable and treatable causes

Published

October 6, 2026

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(targets)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))
pal_fill <- function(...) scale_fill_viridis_d(..., option = "D", end = 0.95)
pal_col  <- function(...) scale_colour_viridis_d(..., option = "D", end = 0.95)

This report describes mort_count, the output of preprocess_mortality().

It assumes one row per death, with the columns sex, area_residenza, asst_2023, eta (age in years), group (cause group) and cause (specific cause). If your file is instead pre-aggregated with a count column n, expand it once with mort_count <- tidyr::uncount(mort_count, n) and everything below works unchanged.

Code 
tar_load(mort_count)
glimpse(mort_count)
Rows: 2,385
Columns: 13
$ sesso                 <fct> M, F, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, F, F, …
$ icd1                  <chr> "C169", "C189", "I679", "I679", "I10", "I10", "C…
$ comune_residenza_2023 <chr> "015205", "015077", "015146", "015146", "015072"…
$ asst_2023             <chr> "20", "21", "26", "26", "22", "22", "20", "24", …
$ nil_2023              <chr> NA, NA, "63", "63", NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, …
$ distretto_2023        <chr> "22011", "22008", "22107", "22107", "22003", "22…
$ eta                   <int> 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74, …
$ causa                 <chr> "C169", "C189", "I679", "I679", "I64", "I64", "C…
$ area_residenza        <chr> "015205", "015077", "015146_63", "015146_63", "0…
$ match_key             <chr> "C16", "C18", "I67", "I67", "I64", "I64", "C34",…
$ group                 <chr> "Cancer", "Cancer", "Diseases of the circulatory…
$ cause                 <chr> "Stomach cancer", "Colorectal cancer", "Cerebrov…
$ weight                <dbl> 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1.0, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5…

Univariate distributions

Sex

Code 
ggplot(mort_count, aes(x = sesso, fill = sesso)) +
  geom_bar() +
  pal_fill(guide = "none") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by sex", x = NULL, y = "Deaths")

Area of residence

Code 
mort_count |>
  mutate(area_residenza = fct_lump_n(area_residenza, n = 25)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_rev(fct_infreq(area_residenza)))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#3b528b") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by area of residence (top 25)", x = "Deaths", y = NULL)

ASST (2023)

Code 
ggplot(mort_count, aes(y = fct_rev(fct_infreq(asst_2023)))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#3b528b") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by ASST (2023)", x = "Deaths", y = NULL)

Age at death

Note that the population is restricted to premature deaths (under 75), so the distribution is bounded at 74.

Code 
ggplot(mort_count, aes(x = eta)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, boundary = 0,
                 fill = "#3b528b", colour = "white") +
  labs(title = "Age-at-death distribution", x = "Age (years)", y = "Deaths")

Cause group

Code 
ggplot(mort_count, aes(y = fct_rev(fct_infreq(group)))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#3b528b") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by cause group", x = "Deaths", y = NULL)

Cause

There are many individual causes, so the 20 most frequent are shown and the remainder collapsed into “Other”.

Code 
mort_count |>
  mutate(cause = fct_lump_n(cause, n = 20)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_rev(fct_infreq(cause)))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#3b528b") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by cause (top 20)", x = "Deaths", y = NULL)

Bivariate distributions

Cause-group composition within each ASST

Each bar is filled to 100%, so it shows the share of deaths attributable to each cause group within an ASST, making the mix comparable across areas of different size.

Code 
ggplot(mort_count, aes(y = fct_rev(fct_infreq(asst_2023)), fill = group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  pal_fill() +
  labs(title = "Cause-group composition within each ASST",
       x = "Share of deaths", y = NULL, fill = "Cause group")

Age pattern of mortality by cause group

Deaths are aggregated to a count per single year of age within each cause group; each point is one age × group cell (alpha = 0.2 to cope with overplotting), and the smoothed line summarises how mortality shifts across the age range — different diseases concentrate at clearly different ages.

Code 
age_group <- mort_count |>
  count(group, eta, name = "deaths")

ggplot(age_group, aes(x = eta, y = deaths, colour = group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, scales = "free_y") +
  pal_col() +
  labs(title = "Age pattern of mortality by cause group",
       x = "Age (years)", y = "Deaths", colour = "Cause group")
